info.name: Tourism Platform API
info.description: Manual test collection for @tourism/api. Two flows split by folder — CUSTOMER and ADMIN — each with sub-folders per area. Auth uses REAL Supabase login (run the 'Login' request in each flow's '00 · Auth' folder first; it captures the bearer token). See apps/api/postman/README.md for setup + prerequisites.

item.name: _SETUP (Admin API · run once)
item.description:
One-time user setup with the Supabase **service_role** key (`{{supabaseServiceRoleKey}}` — fill from `apps/api/.env` → `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`). ⚠️ This key bypasses RLS — never commit or share it. Pushes the passwords in your env onto the Supabase users so Login works.

• FORGOT password on EXISTING users → run **1**, then **3** + **5**.
• FIRST TIME (no users yet) → run **2** + **4** (password is set on create).

request.description: Lists Supabase Auth users; captures {{customerUserId}} / {{adminUserId}} by matching email. Run this first (check the console for the ids).

exec: console.log('customer:', c ? c.id : 'NOT FOUND → run step 2', '| admin:', a ? a.id : 'NOT FOUND → run step 4');

request.description: Creates the customer ALREADY confirmed (no email step). 422 = already exists → use step 3 instead. Captures {{customerUserId}}.

exec: if (pm.response.code < 300) { const u = pm.response.json(); if (u.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('customerUserId', u.id); }

request.description: Resets the EXISTING customer's password to {{customerPassword}} + confirms email. Needs {{customerUserId}} from step 1.
Creates the admin already confirmed. The email must match the API's ADMIN_EMAILS. 422 = exists → use step 5. Captures {{adminUserId}}.

exec: if (pm.response.code < 300) { const u = pm.response.json(); if (u.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('adminUserId', u.id); }

request.description: Resets the EXISTING admin's password to {{adminPassword}} + confirms email. Needs {{adminUserId}} from step 1.

item.name: CUSTOMER
item.description: Customer-facing flow. Auth: Bearer {{customerToken}} (inherited). Run '00 · Auth & Account → Login' first.

request.description: One-time: create the customer in Supabase Auth. If the project requires email confirmation, create the user via the Supabase dashboard instead. 422 = already registered (fine).
Password grant against Supabase GoTrue. Captures access_token → {{customerToken}}.
Mirror the logged-in Supabase identity into the local users table (creates the row keyed by the JWT sub). REQUIRED after first login so other endpoints can resolve the user.
publicId must be a real Cloudinary public_id (get one from Admin → 05 · Media Uploads → signed-url, then upload).

item.name: 01 · Catalog (Public)
item.description: Public browse — No Auth. Run these early: they capture {{tourSlug}}, {{tourId}}, {{categorySlug}}, {{destinationSlug}}, {{departureId}} for downstream requests.

request.description: Creates a PENDING booking. paymentProvider: STRIPE | PAYPAL. Captures {{bookingCode}}.
Returns { checkoutUrl }. Open it in a browser to complete payment (Stripe/PayPal). The webhook then flips the booking to PAID.
PayPal only — call after the buyer approves the order on PayPal's return. STRIPE bookings will 400.
Cancels a PENDING booking you own.

Only a PAID booking owned by the caller can be reviewed (else 400/403). The seeded BK-SEEDPAID belongs to the seed customer, NOT your Supabase-login user — set {{bookingCode}} to a booking you paid for. Duplicate review on the same booking → 409.
tourId is a UUID (captured by Catalog → GET /tours).

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Jane Traveller",
  "email": "jane.lead@example.com",
  "phone": "+44 7700 900123",
  "message": "Interested in a 7-day central Vietnam trip in August for a family.",
  "tourId": "{{tourId}}",
  "nationality": "United Kingdom",
  "travelDate": "2026-08-01",
  "groupSize": 4,
  "budgetTier": "$1000-$2000",
  "interests": ["culture", "food"]
}

request.description: Public lead capture (rate-limited 5/min). Returns { received: true }.
`website` is the honeypot. Still returns 201 (to not tip off bots) but the row is NOT written — verify via Admin → 07 · Enquiries that it never appears.

item.name: ADMIN
item.description: Admin flow. Auth: Bearer {{adminToken}} (inherited). Run '00 · Auth & Stats → Login' first. The admin email MUST be in the API's ADMIN_EMAILS env.

request.description: Mirror + promote to ADMIN. The logged-in email must be present in the API's ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist, else it stays CUSTOMER.
A customer hitting an admin route must be 403.

item.name: 01 · Tours
item.description: Create uses {{categorySlug}} + {{destinationSlug}} — run CUSTOMER → 01 · Catalog first to populate them.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Postman Test Tour",
  "summary": "A tour created from Postman for manual testing.",
  "categorySlug": "{{categorySlug}}",
  "destinationSlugs": ["{{destinationSlug}}"],
  "primaryDestinationSlug": "{{destinationSlug}}",
  "durationDays": 3,
  "maxGroupSize": 16,
  "basePrice": 199,
  "currency": "USD",
  "isPublished": true,
  "suitableFor": ["FAMILY", "COUPLE"],
  "badges": ["BEST_VALUE"],
  "included": ["Guide", "Lunch"],
  "highlights": ["Old town walk"]
}

request.description: Replace-all media set. publicIds must be real Cloudinary public_ids (use 05 · Media Uploads to mint signed URLs and upload first).

item.name: 04 · Departures (nested)
item.description: Nested under an existing tour ({{tourSlug}} from Catalog).

request.description: purpose ∈ TOUR_HERO | TOUR_GALLERY | TOUR_VIDEO | DESTINATION_HERO | DESTINATION_VIDEO | USER_AVATAR. Returns a signed Cloudinary upload payload.
status ∈ NEW | CONTACTED | QUOTED | WON | LOST.

Refunds the seeded PAID booking BK-SEEDPAID (only refundable once → second call 400). The seed's PAID booking has no real provider payment, so the provider refund call may error in a live env; this exercises the route + the atomic seat-release CTE.

item.name: _WEBHOOKS (reference)
item.description: Server-to-server payment webhooks. NOT practical to call manually — they require a valid provider signature over the raw body. To exercise for real: use the Stripe CLI (`stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/v1/payments/stripe/webhook` + `stripe trigger checkout.session.completed`) or the PayPal sandbox webhook simulator. These entries document the endpoints only.

body.raw: { "note": "Use Stripe CLI to deliver a signed event; a hand-written body will be rejected (400, signature invalid)." }
{ "note": "Use the PayPal sandbox webhook simulator; signature is verified against PAYPAL_WEBHOOK_ID." }
